purpose: Post target-blocking GRN scaling check on the real atlas that exposed the 40k->80k wall-clock cliff.
implementation: Worker-local GBM scratch buffers plus target blocking. Target blocking materialises 64 consecutive target genes at a time from row-major expression into a compact column-major target buffer, avoiding one strided full-matrix pass per target gene.
tfs: first 50 bundled human TFs, after expression-matrix resolution
interpretation: The original atlas cliff was dominated by row-major strided target extraction, not just per-target allocator churn. Target blocking changes the 40k->80k segment from an 8.0x wall-clock jump to 2.56x and the full 91,838-cell run from 6,590.6s to 864.1s on the same local atlas setup. The 40k->80k segment remains mildly super-linear, so atlas-scale GRN should be described as near-linear/post-blocking rather than perfectly linear.
